devanagari: राजोवाच । ब्रह्मणा चोदितो ब्रह्मन् गुणाख्यानेऽगुणस्य च । यस्मै यस्मै यथा प्राह नारदो देवदर्शनः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: rājovāca brahmaṇā codito brahman guṇākhyāne yasmai yasmai yathā prāha nārado devadarśanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The king said:
Oh Brahman! Nārada of godly vision[1] (or who makes his followers to visualise God) who was directed by Brahmadeva, to describe in detail the qualities of the Lord, who is free from Sattva and other attributes, expounded to various persons, the real nature of God. Oh prominent one among the knowers of Veda. I would like to know (what is the truth). The episodes of Hari of miraculous powers, are very auspicious to people.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Deva-darśana—One who shows the Supreme Soul (to his followers).

devanagari: एतत् वेदितुमिच्छामि तत्त्वं तत्त्वविदां वर । हरेरद्‍भुतवीर्यस्य कथा लोकसुमङ्गलाः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: etat veditumicchāmi tattvaṃ tattvavidāṃ vara harerad‍bhutavīryasya kathā lokasumaṅgalāḥ
translation:
The king said:
Oh Brahman! Nārada of godly vision[1] (or who makes his followers to visualise God) who was directed by Brahmadeva, to describe in detail the qualities of the Lord, who is free from Sattva and other attributes, expounded to various persons, the real nature of God. Oh prominent one among the knowers of Veda. I would like to know (what is the truth). The episodes of Hari of miraculous powers, are very auspicious to people.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Deva-darśana—One who shows the Supreme Soul (to his followers).

devanagari: कथयस्व महाभाग यथाऽहं अखिलात्मनि । कृष्णे निवेश्य निःसङ्गं मनस्त्यक्ष्ये कलेवरम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kathayasva mahābhāga yathā kṛṣṇe niveśya niḥsaṅgaṃ manastyakṣye kalevaram
translation: Expound to me (those discourses) in such a way that I shall, after fixing my unattached mind on Kṛṣṇa, the antaryāmin (the Soul within all), give up my body.

devanagari: शृण्वतः श्रद्धया नित्यं गृणतश्च स्वचेष्टितम् । कालेन नातिदीर्घेण भगवान्विशते हृदि ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śṛṇvataḥ śraddhayā nityaṃ gṛṇataśca svaceṣṭitam kālena nātidīrgheṇa bhagavānviśate hṛdi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ śru class 5 verb], [ablative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ śru class 5 verb]
[ablative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [genitive single from √ śru class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ ceṣṭ ], [accusative single from √ ceṣṭ ]

translation: The lord enters the heart, within a short time, of those who always hear with faith his deeds and extol the same.

devanagari: प्रविष्टः कर्णरन्ध्रेण स्वानां भावसरोरुहम् । धुनोति शमलं कृष्णः सलिलस्य यथा शरत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: praviṣṭaḥ karṇarandhreṇa svānāṃ bhāvasaroruham dhunoti śamalaṃ kṛṣṇaḥ salilasya yathā śarat
translation: Kṛṣṇa who enters the lotus of his devotee’s heart by way of the ear, cleanses all the sins, just as the season called Śarad (autumnal season) does unto waters (of rivers etc.).

devanagari: धौतात्मा पुरुषः कृष्ण पादमूलं न मुञ्चति । मुक्तसर्वपरिक्लेशः पान्थः स्वशरणं यथा ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dhautātmā puruṣaḥ kṛṣṇa pādamūlaṃ na muñcati muktasarvaparikleśaḥ pānthaḥ svaśaraṇaṃ yathā
translation: A person whose sins are washed away and who becomes free from all troubles (resulting from love, hatred etc.), does not leave the feet of Kṛṣṇa, just as a traveller (who has returned home after a long sojourn) does not leave his home.

devanagari: यदधातुमतो ब्रह्मन्देहारम्भोऽस्य धातुभिः । यदृच्छया हेतुना वा भवन्तो जानते यथा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadadhātumato brahmandehārambho yadṛcchayā hetunā vā bhavanto jānate yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: [2] Oh Brahman! You know really whether it is causeless or with a cause like Karma that the soul, which is not constituted of elements, (still) comes to possess a body made up of elements. Please explain to me the truth about it.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Whether it is due only to the will of God or any other cause like Karman, there happens at the beginning the possession of the body consisting of dhātus (elements), its material cause, the produce of the Prakṛti, in the case of the Soul which is essentially unrelated to dhātus like Prakṛti—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: आसीद् यदुदरात्पद्मं लोकसंस्थानलक्षणम् । यावानयं वै पुरुष इयत्तावयवैः पृथक् । तावानसाविति प्रोक्तः संस्थावयववानिव ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: āsīd yadudarātpadmaṃ lokasaṃsthānalakṣaṇam yāvānayaṃ vai puruṣa iyattāvayavaiḥ pṛthak tāvānasāviti proktaḥ saṃsthāvayavavāniva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: He, (God) from whose navel sprouted forth the lotus which represented the configuration of the worlds, is described as possessing the same form and the same number of limbs as a human being, with the difference that He has limbs of separate and of his own dimensions. (If that be the case) what is the difference between the two (God and his miniature (man)?

devanagari: अजः सृजति भूतानि भूतात्मा यदनुग्रहात् । ददृशे येन तद् रूपं नाभिपद्मसमुद्‍भवः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ajaḥ sṛjati bhūtāni bhūtātmā yadanugrahāt dadṛśe yena tad rūpaṃ nābhipadmasamud‍bhavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The unborn god Brahmā creates beings and controls them through his grace. It is due to his favour that Brahmā (though) born from the lotus (grown) out of his navel could get a glimpse of his form.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī explains verses 8 and 9: At the beginning, during the great deluge (Mahāpralaya), when Nārāyaṇa wished to create the universe, he became the Supreme Man (Parama-puruṣa) and created the Cosmic Egg. He, along with the principles (which led to the creation of the universe) entered the watery portion of the Egg and lay on the bed of Śeṣa. From His navel sprang up the lotus containing fourteen worlds. He came to be called Vairāja within that Egg. ayam puruṣa (this Man) in verse 8 signifies this Cosmic Egg (aṇḍakośa) and is called puruṣa (man) due to God’s presence in the puruṣa form. This Vairāja called Viṣṇu is different from the Egg. But it is due to his presence in the Egg, that the parts of the Egg are represented as his limbs.

devanagari: स चाऽपि यत्र पुरुषो विश्वस्थित्युद्‍भवाप्ययः । मुक्त्वाऽत्ममायां मायेशः शेते सर्वगुहाशयः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sa cā muktvā
translation: In what place does the Supreme Man who dwells in the hearts of all,[4] who creates, protects and destroys the universe, and who is the Lord of māyā, stay when he lays aside[5] his māyā?
purport:
Footnote [4]:
v.l. sarva-guṇāśraya [guṇāśrayaḥ]—Bhāgavata Candrikā: Possessing six excellent qualities fully. Padaratnāvalī: The support of God Brahmā etc. representing Guṇas in the beginning of creation, Or, possessing all excellent qualities such as knowledge, bliss etc.

Footnote [5]:
ātma-māyām muktvā Bhāgavata Candrikā: transcending prakṛti; Padaratnāvalī: Suspending the binding force of prakṛti under his control.

devanagari: पुरुषावयवैर्लोकाः सपालाः पूर्वकल्पिताः । लोकैरमुष्यावयवाः सपालैरिति शुश्रुम ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣāvayavairlokāḥ sapālāḥ pūrvakalpitāḥ lokairamuṣyāvayavāḥ sapālairiti śuśruma
translation: We heard (from you) that the worlds with their protecting deities were created first from the limbs of the Man, and that with these worlds and their presiding deities, the limbs of his body are formed. Please explain this.

devanagari: यावान् कल्पो विकल्पो वा यथा कालोऽनुमीयते । भूतभव्यभवच्छब्द आयुर्मानश्च यत् सतः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvān kalpo vikalpo vā yathā kālo bhūtabhavyabhavacchabda āyurmānaśca yat sataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: What is the duration of Kalpa (Brahmakalpa)? What is the extent of Vikalpa (Manvādi-kalpa)? How is Time measured? What is the significance of the past, present and the future? What is the span of life of men, manes (pitṛs), gods etc.?

devanagari: कालस्यानुगतिर्या तु लक्ष्यतेऽण्वी बृहत्यपि । यावत्यः कर्मगतयो यादृशी द्विजसत्तम ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kālasyānugatiryā tu lakṣyate yāvatyaḥ karmagatayo yādṛśī dvijasattama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ bṛh ], [vocative dual from √ bṛh ], [accusative dual from √ bṛh ], [locative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative dual from √ bṛh ], [vocative dual from √ bṛh ], [accusative dual from √ bṛh ], [locative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ]

translation: What is the nature of Time which, though very long, appears to be very short? What are the places where Karma leads to beings? Of what nature are they, oh the best of Brāhmaṇas?

devanagari: यस्मिन् कर्मसमावायो यथा येनोपगृह्यते । गुणानां गुणिनाश्चैव परिणाममभीप्सताम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yasmin karmasamāvāyo yathā yenopagṛhyate guṇānāṃ guṇināścaiva pariṇāmamabhīpsatām
translation: [6] Please tell me about the individual Souls (lit. possessors of attributes) who wish to become gods etc. as a result of the modifications or changes in the attributes, like sattva, rajas etc.? In what stage does the cumulative effect of good and bad actions take place? By what combinations of actions and by what procedure of doing them is one qualified to get Godhood and such other status.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Explain to me the place where the effect of Karma takes place. Through what agency (of gods etc.) and of what nature is it? Tell us in detail the modifications taking place in attributes and individual Souls and the cause of their attaining the bodies of Gods etc.

devanagari: भूपातालककुब्व्योम ग्रहनक्षत्रभूभृताम् । सरित्समुद्रद्वीपानां सम्भवश्चैतदोकसाम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūpātālakakubvyoma grahanakṣatrabhūbhṛtām saritsamudradvīpānāṃ sambhavaścaitadokasām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: What is the origin of the earth, the nether-world (pātāla), directions, the sky, the planets, the constellations of stars, mountains, rivers, seas and islands? What is the origin of the inhabitants thereof?

devanagari: प्रमाणमण्डकोशस्य बाह्याभ्यन्तरभेदतः । महतां चानुचरितं वर्णाश्रमविनिश्चयः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: pramāṇamaṇḍakośasya bāhyābhyantarabhedataḥ mahatāṃ cānucaritaṃ varṇāśramaviniścayaḥ
translation: What are the dimensions of the Cosmic Egg, separately from within and without? What are the lives and deeds of the great in them? Please tell me definite knowledge about the classes of society and the stages of life (varṇa and āśrama).

devanagari: युगानि युगमानश्च धर्मो यश्च युगे युगे । अवतारानुचरितं यदाश्चर्यतमं हरेः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: yugāni yugamānaśca dharmo yaśca yuge yuge avatārānucaritaṃ yadāścaryatamaṃ hareḥ
translation: What are (the different) Yugas, and what is the extent of each Yuga (age)? What is (the nature of) religion in each Yuga? What are the most miraculous incarnations of Hari and his deeds?

devanagari: नृणां साधारणो धर्मः सविशेषश्च यादृशः । श्रेणीनां राजर्षीणाञ्च धर्मः कृच्छ्रेषु जीवताम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: nṛṇāṃ sādhāraṇo dharmaḥ saviśeṣaśca yādṛśaḥ śreṇīnāṃ rājarṣīṇāñca dharmaḥ kṛcchreṣu jīvatām
translation: What is the course of duties common to men? What are the special duties of guilds of workers and the royal sages? What is the course of conduct to men, who have to live under emergency (āpad-dharma)?

devanagari: तत्त्वानां परिसङ्ख्यानं लक्षणं हेतुलक्षणम् । पुरुषाराधनविधिः योगस्याध्यात्मिकस्य च ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tattvānāṃ parisaṅkhyānaṃ lakṣaṇaṃ hetulakṣaṇam puruṣārādhanavidhiḥ yogasyādhyātmikasya ca
translation: What is the number of tattvas or fundamental principles? What is their characteristic? What is their nature for distinguishing them from their effects? What is the method of worshipping the Supreme Man? And what is the method of practising Yoga consisting of eight stages?

devanagari: योगेश्वरैश्वर्यगतिः लिङ्गभङ्गस्तु योगिनाम् । वेदोपवेदधर्माणां इतिहासपुराणयोः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yogeśvaraiśvaryagatiḥ liṅgabhaṅgastu yoginām vedopavedadharmāṇāṃ itihāsapurāṇayoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: What is the way (like arcir-mārga) of the past masters of Yoga possessing eight miraculous powers? How is the liṅga- śarīra (the subtle body) destroyed? What is the nature of the Vedas (like Ṛgveda, Yajurveda etc.) Upavedas (like Ayurveda, Gandharva-Veda etc.), Dharma (course of conduct) for different classes of society, different stages of life etc., Itihāsa like Mahābhārata) and Purāṇas (18 Mahāpurāṇas and 18 Upapurāṇas)?

devanagari: सम्प्लवः सर्वभूतानां विक्रमः प्रतिसङ्क्रमः । इष्टापूर्तस्य काम्यानां त्रिवर्गस्य च यो विधिः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: samplavaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ vikramaḥ pratisaṅkramaḥ iṣṭāpūrtasya kāmyānāṃ trivargasya ca yo vidhiḥ
translation: What is the nature of the interim deluge or the creation, sustenance and destruction of all the beings? What is the correct method of performing Iṣṭa (maintenance of sacrificial fire, penance, vaiśvadeva etc.) and pūrta (such as construction of temples, lakes etc.) and attaining the triad, viz., dharma, artha and kāma?

devanagari: यश्चानुशायिनां सर्गः पाषण्डस्य च सम्भवः । आत्मनो बन्धमोक्षौ च व्यवस्थानं स्वरूपतः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yaścānuśāyināṃ sargaḥ pāṣaṇḍasya ca sambhavaḥ ātmano bandhamokṣau ca vyavasthānaṃ svarūpataḥ
translation: How are the jīvas (individual souls) who fall from heaven etc., along with the balance of their karmas, born in this world? How did the heretic doctrine come into existence? What is the nature of the so-called bondage and liberation of the Soul? How does the Soul attain to his real essential nature?

devanagari: यथात्मतन्त्रो भगवान् विक्रीडत्यात्ममायया । विसृज्य वा यथा मायां उदास्ते साक्षिवद्विभुः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yathātmatantro bhagavān vikrīḍatyātmamāyayā visṛjya vā yathā māyāṃ udāste sākṣivadvibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: How does the self-dependent lord sport with the help of māyā? How does the all-pervading lord, at the time of the deluge, cast off the māyā and remain aloof as a witness?

devanagari: सर्वमेतच्च भगवन् पृच्छतो मेऽनुपूर्वशः । तत्त्वतोऽर्हस्युदाहर्तुं प्रपन्नाय महामुने ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvametacca bhagavan pṛcchato me tattvato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ praś class 6 verb], [ablative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ praś class 6 verb]
[ablative single from √ praś class 6 verb], [genitive single from √ praś class 6 verb]

translation: Oh venerable great sage? You should kindly explain to me who suppliantly approached you for knowledge, the reality about all these (and other points as well) in the serial order of inquiring.

devanagari: अत्र प्रमाणं हि भवान् परमेष्ठी यथात्मभूः । अपरे चानुतिष्ठन्ति पूर्वेषां पूर्वजैः कृतम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: atra pramāṇaṃ hi bhavān parameṣṭhī yathātmabhūḥ apare cānutiṣṭhanti pūrveṣāṃ pūrvajaiḥ kṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb]
[accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Oh revered one! You are the authority on these just like the great God Brahmā, born of the Supreme Spirit, for other people in this world (blindly) follow what was done by the forefathers of their forefathers.

devanagari: न मेऽसवः परायन्ति ब्रह्मन् अनशनादमी । पिबतोऽच्युतपीयूषं अन्यत्र कुपिताद् द्विजात् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: na me pibato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ pā class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ pā class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ pā class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ pā class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ pā class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [ablative single from √ kup class 10 verb]
[ablative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [ablative single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: Oh Brāhmaṇa! My life (vital airs) will not depart by fasting so long as I drink the nectar (in the form of the stories) of Acyuta, though they may pass away soon due to the wrath of Brāhmaṇa (i.e. the bite by Takṣaka due to the curse).

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । स उपामन्त्रितो राज्ञा कथायामिति सत्पतेः । ब्रह्मरातो भृशं प्रीतो विष्णुरातेन संसदि ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīsūta uvāca sa upāmantrito rājñā kathāyāmiti satpateḥ brahmarāto bhṛśaṃ prīto viṣṇurātena saṃsadi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: Brahmarāta (i.e. Śuka) was highly pleased, when in that assembly, Viṣṇurāta (i.e. Parīkṣit) respectfully requested him, to narrate the episodes of Hari, the Lord of the good (or god Brahmadeva etc.).

devanagari: प्राह भागवतं नाम पुराणं ब्रह्मसम्मितम् । ब्रह्मणे भगवत्प्रोक्तं ब्रह्मकल्प उपागते ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: prāha bhāgavataṃ nāma purāṇaṃ brahmasammitam brahmaṇe bhagavatproktaṃ brahmakalpa upāgate
translation: He expounded him the purāṇa called Bhāgavata which is equivalent to Veda, and which was narrated to god Brahmā by the Lord, at the advent of Brahma-kalpa.

devanagari: यद्यत् परीक्षिदृषभः पाण्डूनामनुपृच्छति । आनुपूर्व्येण तत्सर्वं आख्यातुमुपचक्रमे ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyat parīkṣidṛṣabhaḥ pāṇḍūnāmanupṛcchati ānupūrvyeṇa tatsarvaṃ ākhyātumupacakrame

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Whatever Parīkṣit, the foremost in the Pāṇḍava race, asked, (Śuka) began to narrate in all, in the order of questions.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां संहितायां । द्वितीयस्कंधे अष्टमोऽध्यायः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmadbhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ dvitīyaskaṃdhe aṣṭamo
